task: ALUX AI Agent Intelligence Daily bilingual publish
schedulePolicy: Preserve the existing OpenClaw schedule and timezone; replace only the task workflow.

translationContract.audience: Global AI Agent, infrastructure, technology and business readers
translationContract.style: Native-level technology intelligence publication: professional, concise, accurate and authoritative; never literal word-for-word translation

steps: Pull the latest main branch and inspect git status.
Resume any unfinished translation or review before starting a new date.
Generate the Chinese report in the current reviewed layout and editorial style.

Create a refined en-US translation for global AI Agent, infrastructure, technology and business readers; complete analysis, initial translation, independent review in a separate editor context, and polish, and never translate literally word for word.
Mark the translation reviewed and refresh source/translation hashes.
Build both locales and update both homepages, latest routes, dated routes, archives and sitemap together.
Run structural, hash, link, SEO and multi-viewport rendering checks.
Run the public release boundary scanner and reject private identity, credentials, local paths, internal artifacts and process narration.
Reject every daily change outside the official release allowlist.
Commit Chinese source, English source, translation manifest and public output together; push the official main branch directly without a pull request or manual merge.
Verify the new official domain and deployed file hashes after Vercel finishes deployment, then verify every legacy route uses exactly one permanent redirect to the matching new route.
Only after official-domain verification, send the matching Chinese HTML attachment and the fixed multiline Telegram message.

telegramDelivery.messageTemplate:
【ALUX AI智能体情报日报】

中文站：
https://ai.alux.network/daily/

英文站：
https://ai.alux.network/daily/en/

YYYY-MM-DD：
https://ai.alux.network/daily/YYYY/MM/DD/

atomicReleaseFields: homepage last-updated time derived from the newest reviewedAt
hardGates: No locale may be missing for a published date.
Every translation must be reviewed and match the manifest hashes.
Every translation must receive an independent editorial review outside the initial translation pass before it is marked reviewed.
Every English issue must satisfy the translationContract in full; no stage may be skipped or downgraded to literal or browser machine translation.
Chinese and English external source URL sets and structural counts must match.
No unhandled Chinese text may remain in English content.
No horizontal overflow or clipped content at the required viewport matrix.

Every visible label, badge, heading, URL and control stays inside its owning grid or flex cell and does not intersect a non-ancestor sibling; validation uses rendered text ranges rather than element boxes alone.
English heat-row labels use at least a 172px column above 620px, stack at 620px and below, and never cross into or overlap the content column.
English panel headings and their context labels never overlap, and stack at 920px and below.
The visible logo control and language switch are vertically aligned at 44px high, with language targets at least 44px high.
The rendered heat-row overlap gate must pass and must not be bypassed or weakened.
No file outside repositoryPublishAllowlist may enter a daily release commit.
No private identity, credential, local path, internal artifact, work log, tool output or process narration may enter public release content.
Daily publication must commit and push the official main branch directly; it must not stop at a pull request or wait for a manual merge.
Telegram delivery is blocked until the new official domain serves byte-equivalent current home, latest and dated pages and every legacy route passes the single-308 compatibility gate.
All canonical, hreflang, sitemap, QR-code, share-card and newly published public links must use https://ai.alux.network/daily/ rather than the compatibility domain.
The compatibility domain home, English, latest and dated routes must use exactly one 308 redirect to the matching new route, preserve query parameters, and never produce /daily/daily/.
Routine daily publication must not modify DNS; only the public routing contract belongs in this repository, while provider account and verification data must remain private.
Do not push when any validation fails.
